verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Rey Cortiço After work (16:30 start)
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

best.windFit: W 20 km/h · cross-onshore · poor wind fit
best.bestWind: ideal E/ENE/ESE, usable NE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 19 kJ, 9 s period, lully 3/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at low tide can still work on Belgas if the sandbars are lined up, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 18 kJ, 9 s period, lully 4/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for after work (18:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size
Cerro may feel too soft without more push

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Cantinho da Baia looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 43 kJ, 9 s period, consistent 65/100, and wind is the main risk.  A forgiving sandy bay with lots of take-off points; this is best for easy wave count and cleaner faces when the north wind is bothering exposed beaches; 6-9s at low tide is basically flat or too weak in the sheltered bay; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at low tide is basically flat or too weak in the sheltered bay; Cantinho da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cantinho da Baia may feel too soft without more push; SwellMap rating 1/10 Poor — caution.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is a bit awkward for the bay, so stay cautious.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
6-9s at low tide is basically flat or too weak in the sheltered bay
